_meta.source: memory doc + workspace docs + stakeholders + linked tasks + context_store.current_focus
_meta.note: Point-in-time analysis. Regenerate when refreshing the review. Active cards via parallel analysis agents; on-hold cards carried from prior review.

_overview.headline: The portfolio has one center of gravity: the June 15 CLM cutover. Five top initiatives converge on that date. The wins are real and compounding (Compliance×Product); the danger is in the regulatory items that have gone quiet.

patterns.title: Rollout convergence on June 15
patterns.body: clm-war-room (P0), clm-full-rollout, partners-rollout, and china-hong-kong all converge on the 6/15 100% cutover (~10 days out). Three Critical items — Selfie, Banking, Global/HK E2E — share the same single QA/sandbox environment bottleneck across the war room and full rollout, and that infra blocker is currently unowned. Too much lands on one date with little slack.

patterns.title: Compliance × Product is the repeatable win
patterns.body: The two standout performers — compliance-data-quality (+6.6% CVR, +19% auto-approvals, zero engineering) and t1-localization (4–5× planned impact, 70–90% no-doc onboarding) — are both Compliance×Product partnership plays. T1's 12-week→2-week vendor integration is the scalability unlock. This is the model to templatize and repeat.

patterns.title: Regulatory items: pulled into the light, India now a buffer-less race
patterns.body: The Jun 5 Payoneer-Claude refresh closed both regulatory blind spots. Australia (licenses-regulation) is confirmed DONE — 100% CLM rollout in April, no finding. India is the live danger: actively tracked but on a compressed, no-buffer run to the July 16 license-or-lose deadline (~$25M/yr), with the internal SAR already slipped June 1→~June 22, Export transactions stuck on Citibank, and the cyber-security audit still iterating with Deloitte. The lesson holds — both lost their PM during Yael's leave and went dark until the thread was pulled; the refresh cadence is now the fix, and India needs it weekly until July 16.

patterns.title: P0s tracked in prose, not structure
patterns.body: Nearly every active card flags assigned_agent = NULL and zero linked tasks — including the P0s. The highest-stakes work lives in narrative memory docs rather than trackable owners, dates, and tasks. Systemic hygiene gap across the portfolio.

Global/HK E2E, Selfie dev, 4-step→CLM redirect closeout (clm-full-rollout)
CLM 100% traffic cutover — war room, full rollout, HK→100%, mobile all converge

India hard regulatory SARS deadline incl. cyber-security cert (india-license)

clm-war-room.tldr: P0 operational governance layer for the CLM 100% rollout, commanded by Yonatan with Ira as execution lead and Daniel Grin owning go/no-go. As of the 6/4 standup the program is On-Track on roadmap (56%→100%) but Selfie (R-04) is BLOCKED on a single shared QA/sandbox bottleneck and was due testable by Sun 6/7 — a deadline now at risk with no fresh update, since the memory doc is 1 day stale (last 6/4) against a daily-standup cadence. With the 6/15 cutover ~10 days out, the silence on Selfie and the unowned infra-environment blocker are the two things to verify immediately.

recommendation: Get today's Selfie status now: did the 6/4 12:00 checkpoint pick a workaround, and was Selfie testable by 6/7? This is the gating Critical risk for the 6/15 cutover.
Force an owner onto the infra single-environment / QA-sandbox bottleneck (currently unassigned under Yaron Weiss) — it blocks Selfie, Global-HK, and OCR simultaneously.
Reinstate the daily standup ingest loop — the memory doc went quiet after 6/4 despite a committed daily cadence; with cutover ~10 days out, a 1-day gap is already a signal.
Confirm Daniel's pre-approvable 6/15 go/no-go checklists are produced and Monitoring master (R-11) is on track to be live/green before cutover; chase Einat for the owed Selfie test plan.

claude-kyc-agent.tldr: P0 KYC orchestration layer over foundation-model agents (Anthropic/Google) with a strong early signal: 91% field-extraction accuracy vs Persona's 65% on 45 native-validated Japanese docs. Strategically clear and recently energized — Shilhav now leads day-to-day, framing locked as agnostic-layer (use-not-compete), and direction redirected from accuracy-tuning to deployment. But the entire record is from a single day (2026-06-03); as of 2026-06-05 it is 2 days fresh yet has zero linked tasks, no assigned_agent, and several open action items resting on others (Yaniv's audited set, Omer's doc-access service) with no progress recorded since.

recommendation: Set an assigned_agent and create linked tasks for the four open action items (Yaniv audited-set reproduction, doc-access service, screening-vendor decision, roadmap matrix) so a P0 isn't tracked in prose alone.
Pin a target_date / first-milestone date for the Post-rep QA cell (the 'requires ~nothing' fastest deployment) and the eBay pre-rep rep-shadowing weeks.
Get a status check from Shilhav and Omer in the next few days — the doc-access service is the real bottleneck and nothing has been logged since 2026-06-03.
Confirm the screening/compliance vendor name (UK free API vs US paid aggregator) and the connect-direct vs collect-ourselves decision, which the memory flags as unresolved.

clm-full-rollout.tldr: Fresh and active — memory updated 2026-06-04 (1 day stale) tracking the CLM H1 Strategic Roadmap. Program is On-Track at 56%→100% by the June 15 cutover (10 days out), with July 1 as the committed full-compliance date. Real risk concentration: 3 Critical risk-register items (Banking partnerships, Global/HK E2E, Selfie rollout) plus the still-open 4-step→CLM redirect leak, all due ~Jun 11-15 — a tight stack against a hard deadline. Note governance gap: no formal stakeholders linked in DB and assigned_agent is NULL despite this being a P1 with a live war room.

Assign a tracking agent and link stakeholders (Ira lead, Chen execution) to this P1 in the DB — close the governance gap before the cutover.
Get a firm Jun 11 status on the 4-step→CLM redirect (web/mobile) so the legacy leak is sealed ahead of the Jul 1 full-compliance commitment.
Resolve the unowned Partner Admin mass-registration item and push Chen for the overdue FFT cohort metrics + FFT-definition clarification flagged open since February.

vendor-optimization.tldr: Healthy, actively-tracked P1 owned by Elad Schnarch (assigned_agent: vendor-optimization-pm). Memory is reasonably fresh — last updated 2026-06-03 (2 days stale) with a substantive May 27 PPP. Execution is strong: Au10tix fraud verdict 70%→98%, RAI+Persona to ~100% with fallback, AsiaVerify eKYB production-ready, AIPrise moving to commercial agreement. But the work is now gated almost entirely on internal/legal blockers (on-us go-live), the lone linked task is overdue 11+ weeks, and two material risks (RAI missing 21% of fraud; 69.2% EVS pre-API rejection) have no documented remediation.

Get a read on the Yarden replacement plan and ensure docs-vendor execution continuity isn't at risk during the transition.

ai-academy-product.tldr: The Foundry's Product track is well-designed (14 sessions, dual-track w/ Engineering's 23) but execution has gone quiet. The memory doc last updated Mar 13 and workspace-memory May 3 (33 days stale), and the latest active work is one person — Yonatan personally building Session 12 — not program-wide rollout. The Mar 22-23 announce and ~Apr 26 first-lessons dates are long PASSED with no confirmation they happened; entry/exit criteria (a hard pre-registration blocker) were still undefined as of the last note. No assigned_agent and zero linked tasks mean nothing is actively tracking this.

india-license.tldr: No longer dark — a Jun 5 two-pass refresh (Outlook + SharePoint + Teams) shows India is actively tracked and on a compressed, buffer-less run to the July 16 license-or-lose deadline (~$25M/yr at stake). The internal SAR submission slipped from June 1 → ~June 22; Import flow tested successfully and went to Deloitte (Jun 4), Export is in final testing with FAW2B transactions stuck/rejected on Citibank's side, and the cyber-security/IT/governance audit is still iterating with Deloitte. The V-Team rates India risk 'Low' but there is no schedule slack. FX is resolved (+1.5% buffer treated as a bank fee); Nishant Jaiswal (Sr PM) joined ~May 11 and is ramping alongside Sitara. Still no assigned_agent in our DB.

Lock ownership through the critical six weeks (Sitara↔Nishant on product, Alona→Carrie on licensing), set assigned_agent in our DB, and chase the two unresolved items (2.5M INR cap, Bass escalation) that live in Teams.

t1-localization.tldr: T1 Localization is the portfolio's standout performer: Q1 localized onboarding delivered 4-5x planned impact (+18pp CVR UK, +16pp Brazil vs +4/+2pp plans), 70-90% no-doc onboarding, and approval times cut 52-61%. Memory doc is fresh (updated 2026-06-03, 2 days stale) and PPP shows on-track as of May 27 with Singapore now ahead of plan via AsiaVerify. Watch items: no stakeholder rows or linked tasks beyond one P2 retro, no target_date set, and Yael is on maternity leave with execution running through Eliya Milstein reporting directly to Yonatan. Several Q1 deliverables (POR OCR, US eCollection, Email Reseller US, SG eCollection original plan) slipped or were postponed, though the SG re-plan now looks recoverable.

partners-rollout.tldr: Enterprise partner rollout recovered from RED (May 18) to Medium 🟠 (Jun 3) — Cards shipped May 31, China/HK and strategic partners ramping 10%→25%, and the program is ~90% complete tracking to a June 15 full-completion target. Memory is fresh (updated Jun 3, 2 days stale) and well-sourced from weekly status emails. Bottom line: on the recovery path but not safe — June 8 is a stacked critical window (Airbnb 100%, GBT/Trendyol, Banking) and two live risks (Cards legal opinion pending, Airbnb redirect-flow ambiguity) could re-RED it. No assigned_agent and zero linked tasks despite P1 status.

compliance-data-quality.tldr: Strong, proven initiative: Q1 delivered +6.6% CVR, +19% auto-approvals, and 9% fewer manual reviews via six Compliance×Product policy changes with zero engineering — beating the 3.5% T1-T2 CVR target. But the Q2 set has gone quiet: the latest PPP (May 27) was at-risk with no fresh narrative, eVerification is blocked on EVS, and five Q2 items (Weighted Data Points Scoring, Relax DOI Matching, Remove EU Bank Verification, Fuzzy CVD matching, POCA-as-POR) are past their end-March ETAs but still In Progress/Not Started. Memory was last updated 2026-06-03 (2 days stale — current), but the underlying reporting it cites is itself stale.

licenses-regulation.tldr: Australia — the item that made this look at-risk — is fully resolved after a two-pass Jun 5 gap-close via Payoneer Claude (Outlook + Teams 1:1s). AFSL onboarding via CLM went live ~Apr 20 (50%) and hit 100% ~Apr 27–29 — the Inc path was replaced by AFSL-tagged CLM onboarding, not paused; no regulatory finding. The Mar-3 open items resolved: SPs/eKYC-eKYB vendors disabled (regulation allows governmental-source only) and fell back to docs; LE-for-trusts descoped from M1 (ops manual review). One residual micro-gap: explicit travel-rule sign-off was never confirmed in writing (presumed cleared since full AFSL rollout went live with regulatory alignment). Memory refreshed today. The live risks now sit on the other markets: Israel cards safeguarding monitoring-controls meeting was cancelled and never rescheduled; Canada's regulator-report fix + API testing still open. Still no assigned_agent.

delegated-onboarding.tldr: P1 initiative owned by Meital Lahat Dekter, still in discovery after Yonatan's Mar 29 reset blocked a premature Salesforce/PRM build. Strategy v2 landed Mar 31 and a CSP deck for Yaron was assigned Apr 15 — but the memory doc has been silent for 51 days (last update Apr 15), so current state is unknown and likely drifting. Two CRITICAL risks remain unresolved: no dedicated dev team and unproven CLM step-bypass feasibility, the latter of which can break the whole delegation model. No linked tasks, no PPP swimlane, no Q-plan items — tracking is effectively manual and quiet.

Stand up real tracking — add a PPP swimlane or Q-plan item and refresh the 51-day-stale memory doc so this P1 stops running dark.

china-hong-kong.tldr: China is at 100% rollout and HK at 50%, with Yonatan marking the initiative 'on track' as of 2026-06-03 (2 days stale — current). The bottom line is healthier than the long at-risk history suggests, but real risk remains: the HK eBay rollout is gated (in-house KYC ops may not handle 25% volume; starting at 1-5% pending an eBay test), and the selfie/compliance gap is covered only by manual one-by-one review by Chengdu ops, with selfie slipped from EOQ1 to end of Q2. Tracking is active (assigned to hub-countries-pm, weekly 1:1s with Jojo) but there are no linked tasks in the DB and the formal compliance mapping with Jeff Song/Jessica has been open since Mar 12.

Validate the eBay HK ops readiness for 25% volume before ramping past 1-5%; treat in-house KYC ops capacity as the gating risk.
Create linked tasks (selfie delivery, eBay ramp, compliance mapping) so this P2 has trackable milestones instead of relying solely on 1:1 notes.

ai-native-team-structure.tldr: The thinking is done and genuinely strong — operating model published, hiring process designed, recruitment opened. But execution has stalled: no one is hired (lead PM candidate Denis declined in April), the engineer role is still undefined, and no pod has been stood up. The risk now is the exact thing Yonatan said he didn't want — a brilliant manifesto that gets forgotten. The bias needs to shift from research to commitment. (Now parked / On Hold.)

recommendation: Make the recommendation to Liat — a concrete first-pod proposal (domain + composition + infra prerequisites + success criteria), not a perfected transition playbook. Force a go/no-go.
Unblock the engineer role: a 1-hour decision with Tal/Tomer. It has been "pending" for months and it gates the whole pod.
Refresh the memory doc and confirm the live recruitment pipeline — long dark on a P0 is itself the signal.
Apply the initiative's own thesis to itself: "building the wrong thing perfectly is the new risk" — commit to a real pod before more research.

air-squared.tldr: An initiative whose entire premise is speed has been static for months. It's still pre-staffing and pre-first-experiment: the core team (2 devs + 1 PM) isn't hired, no experiment has shipped, and there's no throughput target. For a "prove by doing" mandate, the gap between the promise (ship fast) and the reality (nothing shipped) is the headline risk — and Oren already rejected the slower infra-framing once. (Now parked / On Hold.)

recommendation: Run one experiment with borrowed hands — use Gaurav's India morning capacity rather than waiting for dedicated headcount. Pick a single bounded problem and run Discovery→MVP in ~2 weeks.
Make the result, not the deck, the resourcing argument to Oren — a shipped internal experiment beats another platform pitch (which he already rejected).
Set a minimal "Beat" target (e.g. 1 experiment to Keep/Toss per N weeks) so throughput is measurable.
Given it's parked, decide explicitly whether it stays P0 — a static speed initiative is a contradiction.

Memory doc last touched 2026-03-02 — ~93 days stale — and has gone quiet since the strong post-workshop momentum. The current_focus shows attention has shifted to successor AI initiatives (AIR², The Foundry/AI Academy, AI-Native Team Structure), with this initiative not in top priorities, suggesting it's being subsumed rather than actively driven.
